headline: These 3 Defense Giants Beat Q1 Estimates—So Why Did Their Stocks Still Fall?
summary: GE Aerospace, Northrop Grumman, and RTX each beat Q1 2026 earnings estimates but sold off after failing to raise full-year guidance, despite analyst Moderate Buy ratings on all three.

headline: Defense Is Less ‘Defensive’ Now. Why Sector Stocks Are Getting Crushed.
summary: The aerospace and defense sector has been a standout lately, free from AI disruption fears and boosted by higher demand for commercial air travel and global defense spending.  Investors probably have some time to wait before the skies clear.  Lockheed Martin  stock was down 3% in midday trading, on pace for its lowest close since January, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

headline: Boeing Earnings Beat as Jet Deliveries Take Off. Stock Rises as Turnaround Takes Shape.
summary: Boeing reported a first-quarter per-share loss of 20 cents from sales of $22.2 billion. Wall Street was looking for a per-share loss of 68 cents from sales of $21.3 billion.

headline: Morgan Stanley Maintains Overweight on Northrop Grumman, Lowers Price Target to $745
summary: Morgan Stanley  analyst Kristine Liwag   maintains Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) with a Overweight and lowers the price target from $765 to $745.

headline: Northrop Grumman Posts Q1 Beat, Stock Tanks On Capex Concerns
summary: Northrop Grumman's stock falls despite upbeat Q1 results, as investors are disappointed by lack of formal B-21 contract renegotiation. Analyst maintains Buy rating and $815 price target, citing company's agreement with Air Force to increase production rate and potential for strong cash generation.

headline: NOC Q1 Deep Dive: Defense Demand, Production Ramps, and Margin Recovery Shape Outlook
summary: Security and aerospace company Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 4.4% year on year to $9.88 billion. The company expects the full year’s revenue to be around $43.75 billion, close to analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $6.14 per share was 1.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: BTIG Reiterates Buy on Northrop Grumman, Maintains $815 Price Target
summary: BTIG  analyst Andre Madrid   reiterates Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) with a Buy and maintains $815 price target.

headline: Northrop Grumman Says Defense Spending Could Reach Cold War Era Levels
summary: Northrop Grumman posts Q1 FY26 earnings and revenue above estimates, driven by strong defense demand and missile systems growth.

summary: The RSI compares a stock's strength on up and down days, indicating short-term performance. Northrop Grumman, TAT Technologies, and Republic Services are oversold with RSI below 30, presenting a buying opportunity.
